Hey everyone, Sam Ovens here and welcome to this training module called Governing Initial Conditions. So in this module, I'm going to explain what to do after you've launched your ads and you've left them for four days. So those four days are crucial. It's like the incubation period for the ads and you have to keep your hands off them during that time. And then on the fourth day, we come in and we look at what's happened. And that's when we start to identify blue swans and red swans and we start to make all of our changes and everything and report all of our data. So this is an extremely important module and it's really where we get to measure whether our stuff has worked or not and what to do about it if it hasn't and what to do about it if it has, like how to really scale things up. So let's get right to it. So here's what we're going to cover in today's training. I'm going to start off with keeping your hands out of Eden and this is our four day no touch rule and this is vital. I'm going to explain why it's vital and how if you really mess this up or if you don't take this advice, nothing you do on Facebook is going to be worth anything. And then we're going to discuss our judgment day pre-check and this is a pre-check which we do before the official judgment day. And this pre-check is basically to check that our ads and our funnel is in order. So we want to make sure that everything's working properly, our ads are getting clicks and impressions and all of that. We also want to make sure our funnel is in order and that's working. And then the official judgment day and this is day four after we've done our pre-check and this is when we use our KPIs to determine the fate of swans. So depending on the KPIs of our different ad sets, we're going to judge them on that fourth day over a period of four days and we're going to see what ones are red swans and what ones are blue swans and we're going to judge them and then we're going to, we're going to call the red ones and we're basically going to do a big cleanup. And then finally, judgment day aftermath. This is after we've done our big cleanup and at this stage we really need to determine whether we need to add more swan DNA variation if necessary. So sometimes after you've gone through and you've done a big, you know, you've done a big purging of everything and you've gotten rid of all of the red swans, sometimes you have enough blue swans left that you just, you can propagate them and then you're good to go and you can hit your targets. But sometimes you have hardly any blue swans left and you really need to add some new DNA variation back into Eden for testing. And once your campaigns show signs of life you want to leave them for a full four days. This is vital and this is what we call keeping your hands out of Eden and this is our four day no touch rule. So as soon as you launch your Facebook ads and get them approved and see signs of life by observing things like impressions, reach and clicks you want to leave your campaigns alone. Do not touch it for four entire days. This is so important. If you come back and touch anything in here you're going to spoil the experiment, you're going to infect it, it's not going to show any accurate data. And you can come back to your ads account every 24 hours just to make sure that you didn't make a mistake and that the ads aren't running or are spending more budget than you had anticipated. Now you shouldn't have done that because these instructions are very thorough and we take you through every step. So it shouldn't have happened if you followed the instructions. And then that's all. That's the only reason why we're coming into check. We're just checking for any sort of emergencies. And we don't want to judge the ad sets. We don't want to judge the ads or the campaign in any way, shape or form. And we don't want to touch or change a single thing. We want to just leave it as is. And these initial four days are extremely important and they're like an incubation period for your ads to interact with the environment and evolve. And ads can look bad in day one and they can be your best ads by day four. So honestly whatever you do don't touch them. You know blue swans, you know you can see ad sets which look like blue swans in day one. And then by day four they're red swans. And you can see red swans looking like, you can see red swans in day one but by day four they're your best blue swans. So there's a lot of stuff going on in these initial four days and that's why we really need to keep our hands off and not go in there and touch anything. We need to let this incubation period play out so that the ad different combinations can you know really interact with the environment and evolve. And that's how we can find what are the good ones, what are the bad ones. This is crucial. And if you do touch your ads account in any way shape or form during these four days you have spoiled the experiment and whatever you think you observe is nothing but false information and you must start over. So be honest with yourself if you do go in and tinker with stuff within the four day period all you have to do is flush everything out. You just have to delete everything you did. And whatever money you spent doesn't matter, whatever results you think you got doesn't matter. You've got to flush it out. You have to let this four day period play out. And it takes a full four days for the algorithm to optimize and iterate between variables. And it also takes four days worth of time, impression, clicks and actions to determine the true KPI numbers since there are ebbs and flows day to day as well as regression to the mean. And no KPI is a KPI till the fourth day. And this is so important. You do not have a KPI on day one. You have a random number. You don't have a KPI on day two. You don't have a KPI on day three. You only KPI's only become KPI's after four days because if you're looking into day if you're looking at the at the isolated days there's going to be so much variance between the different days that you know you're just reporting on false information. You have to let the four days play out. You know there's ebbs and flows and what that means is that you know on things can swing really like 100% day to day. So like if I was running Facebook ads and I was scaled up like one day I might make you know $50,000 a day the next day I might make $100,000 a day and then back to 50 and then back to 100 back to 50 back to 100. And you know that's just how it works. That's just the regression to the mean you know revealing itself and playing playing out. And so this is why it's so important to not isolate individual days because numbers don't behave that way. And if you start judging things and start you know panicking or trying to change things around based on what you see day to day you're going to ruin your whole campaign and just make a big mess. letting them run for four days to really go through that incubation period and then after those four days we're coming to judgment day and that's when we're judging these ad sets to see whether they're red swans or blue swans and then if they're red swans well we're culling them we're turning them off and deleting them and we're taking note of what works and what doesn't work and then we're propagating the blue swans by duplicating them and we're just changing the age in the budget a little bit and then we're adding new swan DNA variations into EDIN for testing and what this means is you know we're going to find other ad angle ideas from our list and we're going to find other audience interest ideas from our list and we're shifting those in so not only are we running some things that are working but we're also duplicating some things that are working we're propagating it and also we're making room for some testing stuff as well so we're kind of juggling three things we're we're continuing to run what's working we're trying to scale up the things that are working we're removing the things that aren't working and then we're adding some things into the mix that are new so that we're always testing and that's really you know the balance of a really solid Facebook campaign management it's scaling the proven winners culling the the things that aren't working flushing it out and then having some room for constant testing and variation that is how you want to run your campaign and that is really how you govern the initial conditions that's how you you let it run for four days then you come in and you observe everything and then you can make all of those changes on the fourth day and as you can see you know when we wait until the fourth day there's quite a lot of stuff we can actually do we can get a lot of metrics we can get a lot of insights we can you know see what's worked and what hasn't worked and we can actually start scaling some things up cutting some things back and even bringing some new things into the mix for experimentation now that's the beauty of waiting four days if we'd only waited one day we'd probably just look at our numbers and then we'd probably feel nervous or anxious or something and it might not be for any good reason at all because we don't even know what we're looking at and that's why it's so important to give them those four days it's really takes that long for the numbers to really you know to really define themselves and and show and before that period of four days you're not really looking at at anything you're just looking at a whole bunch of noise and so that's why that four-day rule is vitally important so the next step is to achieve omnipresence so achieving omnipresence launching your retargeting
